violations.text: Staff failed to wash and sanitize equipment (saucepan) after rinsing in the 3 bay sink. Proper washing, rinsing, sanitizing of equipment was reviewed with the PIC at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Dry ingredient items were observed stored unprotected/uncovered. PIC made aware food items are to be protected from possible contamination when opened or removed from original packaging. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The following in-use utensils were observed stored improperly: - Several knives were observed stored in unclean areas of prep unit and stored between countertop and wall. Knives/utensils are to be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location between uses. - Scoops/pans observed stored in dry ingredients with handles not above the food. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Utensils were rearranged for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The following equipment was observed in poor repair or adjusted for alternate use: - Single use containers with lids were observed cut and used as shaker containers. - Inspector observed plastic bottles cut in a way to be used as scoops. PIC to obtain proper equipment to be used in the manner intended that is smooth, durable, easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2026

The sink used for food preparation is lacking a proper air gap (currently equipped with an air break). The drainpipe to the prep sink is to be adjusted so it does not end sitting into or below the flood rim of the drainpipe. Air gap document printed and left with the PIC at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter not necessary to the establishment to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

The floor in the basement area was observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, old droppings, and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/08/2026

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

Soup was observed held at 125 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated soup at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back area, was kept open with no additional form of protection from pest entry. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024
Several knives were observed stored in unclean area of prep unit and counter tops. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

Inspector observed a scoop made from cutting a plastic bottle. This equipment is not considered to be smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. PIC instructed to discontinue its use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

The food prep sink is lacking an adequate air gap. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

The floor in the basement storage area has an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024
